title: Quiz: Quantum Noise
description: Test your understanding of quantum noise sources and their effects on quantum systems

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary source of quantum noise?
questions.explanation: Entanglement with other qubits in the computer is a designed feature, not noise. Noise comes from unwanted interactions with the environment. Thermal fluctuations, electromagnetic interference, and control errors are all sources of noise.

questions.question: What is the purity Tr(rho^2) of a maximally mixed single-qubit state?
questions.explanation: For a maximally mixed single-qubit state rho = I/2, we have Tr(rho^2) = Tr(I/4) = 1/2. Pure states have purity 1, while mixed states have purity less than 1.

questions.question: A bit-flip (X) error on the state |+> = (|0> + |1>)/sqrt(2) results in:
questions.explanation: The |+> state is an eigenstate of the X operator with eigenvalue +1. Applying X to |+> gives X|+> = |+>. This is why |+> is immune to bit-flip errors but sensitive to phase-flip (Z) errors.

questions.question: The depolarizing channel with parameter p=1 transforms any input state to:
questions.explanation: With p=1, the depolarizing channel applies X, Y, and Z errors each with probability 1/3. This completely randomizes the state, resulting in the maximally mixed state rho = I/2.

questions.question: Coherent errors are generally preferable to incoherent errors because they are easier to correct through calibration.
questions.explanation: True! Coherent errors are systematic and predictable, so they can often be characterized and corrected through calibration or active correction. Incoherent errors are random and require error correction codes.

questions.question: How do coherent rotation errors accumulate over n gates?

options.text: The error grows linearly as n

questions.explanation: Coherent errors accumulate linearly because they always push the state in the same direction. If each gate has rotation error epsilon, after n gates the total error is n*epsilon.

questions.question: Which Pauli operator represents a combined bit-flip and phase-flip error?
questions.explanation: The Y operator equals iXZ (up to a global phase), representing a simultaneous bit-flip and phase-flip. It takes |0> to i|1> and |1> to -i|0>.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about density matrices are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.text: The trace of a density matrix equals 1
Density matrices can have negative eigenvalues

questions.explanation: Density matrices are Hermitian (a), have trace 1 (b), and pure states have purity Tr(rho^2) = 1 (c). Density matrices are positive semi-definite, so they cannot have negative eigenvalues (d is false).
